items.headline: U.S. Stocks Rebound as Investors Digest Fed's Hawkish Stance and Iran Peace Deal
items.summary: U.S. equity markets saw a rebound on Thursday, recovering a significant portion of losses from Wednesday's session. The Federal Reserve's decision to hold interest rates steady but project potential future hikes continues to be a key factor, while the signing of an interim peace deal between the U.S. and Iran provided a boost to sentiment, particularly impacting oil prices and chipmakers.

items.headline: Hive Jumps on AI Infrastructure Partnership
items.summary: Hive Digital Technologies (HIVE) saw its shares climb after its subsidiary announced a partnership with Bell Canada, Cohere, and Hypertec to develop AI infrastructure in Canada.

items.headline: Rumble Surges on AI Business Unit Plans
items.summary: Online video network platform Rumble (RUM) jumped after revealing plans to operate two core business units, including a new cloud and AI-infrastructure business, Quake AI.

items.headline: Intel Rallies on Apple Chip Design Partnership
items.summary: Intel shares soared in premarket trading after President Trump announced the company would partner with Apple to design chips in the U.S.

items.headline: NovoCure Tumbles in Premarket
items.summary: NovoCure Limited (NVCR) experienced a significant premarket tumble.

items.headline: Accenture Falls on Disappointing Revenue Forecast
items.summary: Accenture (ACN) saw a substantial drop in premarket trading after providing a fourth-quarter revenue forecast that fell short of Wall Street's expectations.

items.headline: IBM Declines Amid Market Movement
items.summary: IBM shares experienced losses during Thursday's trading session.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Hints at Future Hikes
items.summary: The Federal Reserve, under new Chairman Kevin Warsh, maintained its benchmark interest rate at 3.50-3.75%. However, updated projections from policymakers indicated a growing possibility of further rate increases later this year, shifting the median Fed funds rate forecast to 3.8% from 3.4%.

items.headline: U.S. and Iran Sign Interim Peace Deal, Oil Prices Retreat
items.summary: An interim peace agreement was signed between the United States and Iran, leading to a de-escalation of conflict and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz to oil tanker traffic. This development contributed to a retreat in crude oil prices.

items.headline: May Retail Sales Exceed Expectations
items.summary: The Department of Commerce reported that U.S. retail sales rose by 0.9% in May, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of 0.4%. Core retail sales, excluding automobiles, also saw a notable increase of 0.8%.

items.headline: Kroger Reports Q1 2026 Results
items.summary: The Kroger Co. ($KR) announced its first quarter 2026 results today. The company reported a 1.0% increase in identical sales without fuel, an operating profit of $1,407 million, and an EPS of $1.46. Adjusted EPS reached $1.58.

items.headline: Chipmakers and AI Stocks Lead Thursday's Gains
items.summary: Following a session where all S&P 500 sectors closed lower, chipmakers and artificial intelligence-related stocks rallied on Thursday, leading the broader market higher. This highlights a continued investor focus on high-growth technology sectors.
